Package microui.core.base

Class View

java.lang.Object
microui.core.base.View
All Implemented Interfaces:
Visible
Direct Known Subclasses:
AbstractShadow, ContainerManager, Hover, Ripples, SpatialView, Tooltip, TooltipManager
public abstract class View extends Object implements Visible
Abstract base class for all visual elements in MicroUI. Provides fundamental functionality for managing visibility, rendering priority, and element identification within the GUI framework.

The View class serves as the foundation for all visual components in MicroUI. Subclasses must implement the render() method to define their specific drawing logic. This class implements the Visible interface and provides built-in support for metrics collection.

Key features include: - Visibility management (show/hide) - Rendering priority control (z-order) - Numeric and text-based identification - Integration with MicroUI's rendering system - Automatic style management via push/pop

Status: STABLE - Do not modify Last Reviewed: 29.10.2025

  • Constructor Details

    • View

      public View()
      Constructs a new View with default values. Automatically registers the view with the metrics system.

  • Method Details

    • isVisible

      public final boolean isVisible()
      Checks whether the element is visible.
      Specified by:
      isVisible in interface Visible
      Returns:
      true if the element is visible, false if hidden

    • setVisible

      public final void setVisible(boolean visible)
      Sets the visibility of the element.
      Specified by:
      setVisible in interface Visible
      Parameters:
      visible - true to show the element, false to hide

    • getPriority

      public final int getPriority()
      Returns the priority of rendering the element. Elements with a higher priority are drawn on top of others.
      Returns:
      current rendering priority (в‰Ґ 0)

    • setPriority

      public final View setPriority(int priority)
      Sets the priority of rendering the element.
      Parameters:
      priority - new rendering priority (must be в‰Ґ 0)
      Returns:
      this View for method chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if priority is less than 0

    • getId

      public final int getId()
      Returns the numeric identifier of the element.
      Returns:
      numeric element Id (в‰Ґ 0)

    • setId

      public final View setId(int id)
      Sets the numeric identifier of the element.
      Parameters:
      id - new identifier (must be between MIN_ID (0) and MAX_ID (Integer.MAX_VALUE))
      Returns:
      this View for method chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the identifier is less than 0

    • getTextId

      public final String getTextId()
      Returns the text identifier of the element.
      Returns:
      text id of the View object

    • setTextId

      public final View setTextId(String textId)
      Sets text identifier for this View object.
      Parameters:
      textId - new text identifier for this View object (cannot be null and (or) empty)
      Returns:
      this View for method chaining
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if textId is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if textId is blank

    • hasId

      public final boolean hasId()
      Checks if this View has a non-default numeric identifier.
      Returns:
      true if id is not equal to DEFAULT_ID (0), false otherwise

    • hasTextId

      public final boolean hasTextId()
      Checks if this View has a non-default text identifier.
      Returns:
      true if text id is not equal to DEFAULT_TEXT_ID (""), false otherwise

    • draw

      public void draw()
      Renders the element if it is visible. Automatically manages Processing styles (push/pop style).

      In STRICT render mode, this method validates that drawing is performed within the proper context (ContainerManager must be initialized and drawing must occur during ContainerManager's draw cycle).

      Calls the method only if the element is visible.

      Throws:
      RenderException - if in STRICT mode and ContainerManager is not initialized or if drawing occurs outside ContainerManager's draw cycle
